pi_items.source: Task configuration choice - verified from task.json description
GeoServer setup creates 'ne' workspace per setup_geoserver.sh line 137
GeoServer setup publishes Natural Earth ne_110m_populated_places as 'ne_populated_places' per setup_geoserver.sh line 120, 186
Task description and verifier.py line 16 confirm formula: Size = 6 + (pop_max / 1000000)
Task description and verifier.py line 16 confirm formula: Size = 6 + (pop_max / 1000000)
Confirmed via direct inspection of Natural Earth 110m populated places DBF file - POP_MAX field exists with values ranging from 500 to 35,676,000
Valid hex color code for orange (RGB 255,153,0) - verified through hex parsing. Confirmed in verifier.py line 93-94
Valid hex color code for black (RGB 0,0,0) - verified through hex parsing. Confirmed in verifier.py line 95-96

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real public data from Natural Earth 110m populated places dataset. The target layer 'ne_populated_places' is created from the official Natural Earth shapefile (ne_110m_populated_places) which contains 243 cities with genuine metropolitan population data (pop_max attribute). This attribute contains real values ranging from 500 to 35.6 million people. The graduated symbol formula (Size = 6 + pop_max/1,000,000) produces cartographically reasonable symbol sizes from 6.0 to 41.68 pixels. The specified colors (#ff9900 orange and #000000 black) are valid hex color codes. The workspace 'ne' and layer 'ne_populated_places' are set up by the environment initialization scripts using official Natural Earth data sources.
